# The Shape of the Thing

The Shape of the Thing

Where we are right now, and what likely happens next

title: AI agents fail from lack of context, not lack of smarts
author: Manish Garg
summary: AI agents fail not because models are weak but because enterprise context is missing. Most companies deploy agents into fragmented data, inconsistent workflows, and siloed logic.
why_it_matters: This is directly relevant to your AI-native intrapreneur positioning because it reinforces that workflow context and operational reality matter more than model hype.
core_claim: AI agents fail not because models are weak but because enterprise context is missing.
headline_candidates: AI agents fail not because models are weak but because enterprise context is missing.
supporting_claims: Most companies deploy agents into fragmented data, inconsistent workflows, and siloed logic.
Before blaming the model, invest in context capture so agents can act safely and intelligently inside the real business.
body_text:
# AI agents fail from lack of context, not lack of smarts

AI agents fail not because models are weak but because enterprise context is missing.

Most companies deploy agents into fragmented data, inconsistent workflows, and siloed logic.

Before blaming the model, invest in context capture so agents can act safely and intelligently inside the real business.

title: Admissions teams are a content goldmine
author: Marc Zarefsky
summary: If you want better higher ed content, start with your admissions team. They hear the real questions prospects ask.
why_it_matters: This is directly useful for your role because it ties admissions, outreach, and content strategy together in a way that is practical and employer-aligned.
core_claim: If you want better higher ed content, start with your admissions team.
headline_candidates: If you want better higher ed content, start with your admissions team.
supporting_claims: They hear the real questions prospects ask.
Those conversations can become landing pages, alumni stories, and content that actually helps prospective students make decisions.
body_text:
# Admissions teams are a content goldmine

If you want better higher ed content, start with your admissions team.

They hear the real questions prospects ask.

Those conversations can become landing pages, alumni stories, and content that actually helps prospective students make decisions.

title: AI will not replace higher education, but it can augment it
author: Esther Harclerode
summary: AI will not replace higher education because people still need human connection and many forms of learning require human practice, review, and interpretation. AI can augment parts of the work, but humans still need to teach, correct, connect, and make meaning.
why_it_matters: This gives you a role-safe education lens for AI content: human connection stays central, while AI should improve how the work gets done.
core_claim: AI can augment parts of the work, but humans still need to teach, correct, connect, and make meaning.
headline_candidates: AI can augment parts of the work, but humans still need to teach, correct, connect, and make meaning.
supporting_claims: AI will not replace higher education because people still need human connection and many forms of learning require human practice, review, and interpretation.
Humans still need to teach, correct, connect, and make meaning.
body_text:
# AI will not replace higher education, but it can augment it

AI will not replace higher education because people still need human connection and many forms of learning require human practice, review, and interpretation.

AI can augment parts of the work, but humans still need to teach, correct, connect, and make meaning.

title: The Ezra Klein Show: How Fast Will A.I. Agents Rip Through the Economy?
author: hardfork@nytimes.com (The New York Times)
summary: The “Hard Fork” team is off this week, taking a much-needed break.
